proof: function[T0](x0: FiniteSet[T0], x1: FiniteSet[T0]) { x0.underlying_set ⊂ x1.underlying_set = x0 ⊆ x1 }[T](s, t)

goal: exists(k0: List[T]) { fs_from_list[T](k0) = fs_difference[T](t, s) and k0.is_unique and k0.length = Nat.0 }

proof: function[T0](x0: T0, x1: List[T0], x2: List[T0]) { List.cons[T0](x0, x1) != x2 or x1.length.suc = x2.length }[T](head, tail, diff_items)
function[T0](x0: FiniteSet[T0], x1: T0) { x1 ∈ x0.underlying_set = x1 ∈ x0 }[T](t, x)

function[T0](x0: FiniteSet[T0], x1: lib(set_base).Set[T0]) { Option.some[FiniteSet[T0]](x0) != FiniteSet.new[T0](x1) or x0.underlying_set = x1 }[T](fs_from_list[T](diff_items), list_set[T](diff_items))
function[T0](x0: FiniteSet[T0], x1: T0) { x1 ∈ x0.underlying_set = x1 ∈ x0 }[T](fs_from_list[T](diff_items), x)
function[T0](x0: List[T0], x1: T0) { not x1 ∈ x0 or List.nil[T0] != x0 }[T](List.nil[T], x)
function[T0](x0: FiniteSet[T0], x1: T0) { x1 ∈ x0.underlying_set = x1 ∈ x0 }[T](s, x)
let w0: T satisfy { w0 ∈ t.underlying_set and not w0 ∈ s.underlying_set }
function(x0: T) { not x0 ∈ t.underlying_set or x0 ∈ s.underlying_set }(w0)
